entity.summary: South Korean centralized cryptocurrency exchange operating under the Bithumb brand. In February 2026 it accidentally credited users with approximately 620,000 BTC through an internal promotional-payout and ledger error, temporarily restricted affected accounts, recovered most erroneous balances, and remained active under regulatory inspection.
entity.notes: Do not mark dead or hacked. The February 2026 incident is already represented by canonical operational-error, account-restriction, and regulatory-review events and is not duplicated here. Official anniversary and current service pages confirm continued operation; the stored 2014 exchange-launch marker is retained because anniversary language alone does not establish a more precise trading-service launch date.

expected.summary: South Korean centralized cryptocurrency exchange. In February 2026 it accidentally credited users with approximately 620,000 BTC in an internal promotional payout / ledger error, temporarily restricted affected accounts, recovered most erroneous balances, and came under regulatory inspection. The exchange remained active.
expected.notes: Do not mark dead or hacked. The February 2026 incident was reported as an internal ledger / promotional payout error rather than an external theft. Track it as a severe operational incident with affected-account restrictions and regulatory review while keeping the entity active.

evidence.title: Bithumb 11th anniversary trading-fee event announcement
evidence.notes: First-party anniversary announcement confirming long-running Bithumb brand and platform continuity.
